methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Hystar is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 6 same-stage, same-sector companies, supplemented by 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers in the Climate & Energy space, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $36M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against 6 same-stage peers in Climate & Energy, which show a median valuation of $355M with a range from $120M to $5.0B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in the peer set, with Commonwealth Fusion Systems at the high end ($5.0B) and unnamed peers near the low end.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $58M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $212M. Stage-progression favors the lower bound, while peer-median anchors toward the higher range. Key uncertainties include which peer comparables best reflect Hystar's technology maturity, capital efficiency, and addressable market relative to the broader Climate & Energy cohort. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—spanning nearly 40x—suggests that stage and sector alone do not fully determine value; execution, intellectual property, and go-to-market strategy merit granular assessment.
